questions.stem: Which of the following primitive data types can store the largest whole-number value?
questions.explanation: D Among these, long has the largest range of whole numbers (64-bit vs 32-bit int, 16-bit short, 8- bit byte).

questions.stem: Which statement is always true in Java for any int x?
questions.explanation: B In two’s complement, bitwise NOT is equivalent to negating and subtracting one, so ~x always equals -x - 1.

questions.stem: Which of the following statements about Java primitive data types is TRUE?

choices.A: A byte can store values from -128 to 128.
choices.B: When a short with value 32767 is incremented, it becomes 32768.
choices.C: A long and a double both use 32 bits of memory.
choices.D: When a byte with value -128 is decremented, it becomes 127.
choices.E: An int can store any value that a long can store.

questions.explanation: D When a byte at its minimum value -128 is decremented, it underflows and wraps to the maximum value 127; A is false because byte ranges from -128 to 127 (not 128); B is false because 32767 wraps to -32768; C is false because both long and double use 64 bits; E is false because long is 64 bits and can store larger values than the 32-bit int.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment shown on the right?
questions.explanation: E Byte.MAX_VALUE = 127 Byte.SIZE = 8 127/8 = 15

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment shown on the right?
questions.explanation: D int A = Byte.MAX_VALUE; int B = Byte.MIN_VALUE; out.print(A - B); 127 - (-128) = 255

questions.stem: Which of the following is the correct order of floating-point values smallest to largest?

choices.A: negative infinity, finite negative values, negative zero, zero, finite positive values, infinity
choices.B: negative infinity, finite negative values, zero, negative zero, finite positive values, infinity

questions.explanation: C Negative zero and zero are treated as equivalent.

questions.stem: What is the output of the code segment shown on the right?
questions.explanation: C int K = Integer.SIZE; 32 int L = Double.SIZE; 64 int M = Byte.SIZE; 8 out.println(K / M + L / K); 32/8 + 64/32 = 4+2 = 6
